summary: Extended the Path Map Event Proof lens with NEWER/OLDER full-trail navigation and a position readout so operators can walk neighboring lane events without leaving Node Intel.

queries: timeline event detail next previous navigation UX pattern
activity feed event detail previous next dashboard UX
master detail previous next record navigation dashboard UI
pattern_notes: Previous and next record controls reduce repeated back-and-forth when reviewing many records.
Timeline navigation should preserve context and stay responsive rather than forcing a separate page.
Dense activity feeds need compact controls that do not expand the first scan or break small cards.

data_inputs: focused Path Map Node Intel event
ui_contract: Resolve the selected proof event against the full lane trail, not only the visible related-event stack.
Show the selected event's 1-based trail position and total lane trail length.
Add NEWER and OLDER controls that update browser-local selected event state.
Pull an out-of-stack selected event into the visible related-event list so the lens and selected row stay connected.
Keep the compact nav row contained inside narrow Node Intel cards across responsive grid widths.

debugging.issue: Initial green check found the Route Handoff card intercepting the OLDER button click.
debugging.root_cause: The Event Proof lens and nav row were contributing an intrinsic width wider than the narrow Node Intel grid column, causing the OLDER control to overflow into the neighboring card.
debugging.fix: Constrained Node Intel children, forced the lens to a minmax(0, 1fr) grid track, made the nav row shrinkable, and reduced nav button height/padding.

red_route.evidence: Before implementation, .path-event-nav was absent, there were 0 data-path-event-step buttons, no .path-event-position, and no NEWER/OLDER labels.

green_route.evidence: The lens rendered 2 nav buttons and position 53 / 64. Clicking OLDER changed the position to 54 / 64 and changed the lens title to Browser Research while keeping exactly 1 selected event and no browser errors.

responsive.evidence: All tested viewports completed an OLDER step to 54 / 64, kept one selected event, kept document width equal to viewport width, and reported no bad rectangles, console errors, or failed requests.
